title: Major FX Pairs Rally on Dollar Weakness: Risk-On Sentiment Dominates Asian Session
intro: The US Dollar exhibited broad-based weakness during Asian trading hours as risk-on appetite dominated early May flows. The Dollar Strength Index declined to -0.923 amid continued speculation over Federal Reserve policy divergence. The Japanese Yen led gains among major currencies against the greenback, while commodity currencies surged sharply on renewed optimism regarding economic growth and rising commodity prices.

sections.heading: Currency Strength Rankings and Market Dynamics
sections.content: Market leadership emerged from the antipodean currencies, with NZD posting intraday gains of +0.94%, followed by AUD at +0.90%, supported by hawkish RBNZ rhetoric driving flows into risk-sensitive assets. Sterling advanced +0.77% on optimism surrounding UK economic data, while USD/JPY weakness contributed to Yen strength across all crosses. The Canadian Dollar gained +0.62% supported by oil price recovery, while EUR demonstrated modest gains of +0.38%. The Swiss Franc strengthened +1.03% against USD amid safe-haven demand, though JPY emerged as the strongest performer against the Dollar with a -1.82% USD decline. This currency rotation reflects a fundamental shift in risk appetite, with investors repositioning away from traditional safe havens toward growth-sensitive currencies.

sections.heading: Major Currency Pairs Technical Analysis and Price Action
sections.content: EUR/USD extended gains above 1.17, reaching 1.1729, supported by ECB policy normalization expectations with immediate resistance at 1.1750 maintaining bullish sentiment. GBP/USD advanced toward 1.36, trading at 1.3593 on Bank of England tightening speculation, where a break above 1.3600 targets 1.3650 under constructive outlook scenarios. USD/JPY retreated from multi-year highs to 157.2720 due to intensifying currency intervention fears, with critical support at 157.00 and bearish momentum building below 158.00. AUD/USD continued recovery above the 0.72 threshold at 0.7195 on China growth optimism, targeting resistance at 0.7250. USD/CHF faced Franc strength pressure below mid-0.78 levels at 0.7825, where support at 0.7800 remains crucial for Dollar bulls. USD/CAD declined to 1.3586 as oil price gains strengthened the Loonie toward 1.3550 support, with bearish momentum favoring further CAD strength. NZD/USD approached 0.59 at 0.5896 on hawkish RBNZ pivot expectations, where a break above 0.5900 opens targets at 0.5950.

sections.heading: Cross-Currency Rotation and Flow Analysis
sections.content: Currency cross dynamics revealed significant rotational patterns with EUR/JPY retreating from highs to 184.4210 as Yen strength dominated, testing 184.00 support levels. GBP/JPY pulled back from 215.00 resistance to 213.7730 on profit-taking, while AUD/JPY consolidated at 113.1150 despite broader Yen strength. EUR/GBP declined to 0.8625 as Sterling outperformed the Euro on relative yield dynamics, and CAD/JPY weakened to 115.7700 on broad Yen recovery. Flow analysis indicates accelerating carry trade unwinding as USD/JPY retreat triggers systematic selling, while commodity currency positioning turns net long as industrial metals rally supports AUD and CAD. Month-end rebalancing flows favor risk currencies over traditional safe havens, with Asian sovereign reserve managers accumulating EUR/USD on ECB policy divergence expectations.

sections.heading: Trading Opportunities and Risk Assessment
sections.content: Intraday trading opportunities include long AUD/USD targeting 0.7250 on China PMI optimism and commodity strength, long NZD/USD on 0.5900 breakout amid hawkish RBNZ sentiment, and EUR/CHF recovery toward 0.9200 on risk-on flows. Short opportunities encompass USD/JPY toward 156.50 on intervention fears, USD/CAD testing 1.3550 on oil rally, and EUR/GBP continuation toward 0.8600 on Bank of England premium. Key macroeconomic drivers include China Manufacturing PMI data during Asian session and expected US ISM data. ECB speakers Lagarde and Schnabel will provide monetary policy guidance, alongside Australian building permits and Japanese consumer confidence data. Primary risk factors include heightened Bank of Japan intervention threats below USD/JPY 157.00 creating volatility spikes, potential Chinese policy disappointment rapidly reversing commodity currency gains, and Federal Reserve pushback against dovish market expectations threatening Dollar recovery across all major pairs.

quote: The Dollar Strength Index declined to -0.923 amid continued speculation over Federal Reserve policy divergence, with JPY emerging strongest at -1.82% USD decline.
conclusion: Major FX pairs exhibit clear directional bias toward risk-on positioning, with commodity currencies leading gains against broad Dollar weakness. Technical levels suggest continuation patterns across most majors, though intervention risks in USD/JPY and potential Fed policy surprises represent key downside catalysts. The 70% probability scenario favors further Dollar decline against commodity currencies, while ECB policy divergence supports EUR strength. Traders should monitor China PMI data and central bank communications for confirmation of current directional trends.
excerpt: Dollar weakness drives major FX pairs higher as risk-on sentiment dominates Asian trading session flows.
